mechanism_claim: All-in-one bottom-bracket area drive unit with automatic transmission, two electric motors, belts, planetary gearing, ratchets/freewheel and shared PCB.
control_claim: Supports integrated assistance and automatic ratio management in one module.
parameters: Gen1 public claims: 600 W peak, 65-120 Nm, 265% ratio range, 4.6 kg.
usable_for_model: Architecture topology, baseline ratio range, mass and peak torque can seed a low-power model.
feasibility_implication: Strongest public evidence that a two-motor e-bike eCVT-like module can be packaged and commercialized in an urban bike class.
open_questions: No public efficiency map, thermal derating, ratio-rate limit, high-power durability or fault-degradation matrix.

mechanism_claim: No derailleur or gear hub; variation is integrated with electric assistance and gear-change management.
control_claim: User selects cadence and the system changes ratio continuously to hold it.
parameters: Target cadence operation; two motors; Decathlon LD 920 E first equipped bike.
usable_for_model: Can support target-cadence controller definition and UX boundary.
feasibility_implication: Cruise target-cadence logic is real in a mature city-bike implementation.
open_questions: Does not prove high-torque eMTB/cargo feel, sprint intent detection or climb torque-comfort control.

mechanism_claim: Retail OEM-fit evidence for an automatic Owuru motor city bike.
control_claim: Product existence confirms automatic cadence route reached market in at least one city-bike platform.
parameters: Retail model evidence; availability varies by region/time.
usable_for_model: Can anchor commercialization maturity level for low-power target-cadence UX.
feasibility_implication: Proves target cadence is not only a concept-page claim.
open_questions: Retail page does not expose internal mechanism, efficiency, thermal or failure mode.

mechanism_claim: Reports high-power integrated continuous-variation motor route for eMTB/cargo.
control_claim: Reports target-cadence operation in a high-power prototype context.
parameters: Reported prototype figures: 150 Nm, 1000 W peak, 3.9 kg, 500% range.
usable_for_model: Only usable as prototype parameter candidate; must be treated as medium evidence.
feasibility_implication: Shows high-power eCVT MGU direction is being pursued, but not proven as production-stable.
open_questions: Need official datasheet, test conditions, efficiency, heat and durability.

mechanism_claim: Traction CVP hub/gearbox route with continuously variable ratio and enclosed drivetrain compatibility.
control_claim: AUTOMATiQ preferred pace/cadence control supports automatic target-cadence UX.
parameters: Public product ranges include 310-400% family-level ratio ranges; torque/GVW limits vary by model.
usable_for_model: Can model traction-CVP architecture as mature target-cadence substitute with ratio range and load envelope placeholders.
feasibility_implication: Strong proof that preferred-cadence CVT behavior is mature outside motor-internal MGU.
open_questions: Hub/gearbox route does not prove mid-drive eCVT MGU thermal, packaging or torque density.

mechanism_claim: Motor and 9/12-speed sealed gearbox integrated in one unit; not continuous CVT.
control_claim: Auto.Shift functions target preferred cadence; load shifting and start/coast functions are public product features.
parameters: Public specs include 85 Nm, 600/800 W peak, 568/600% range, around 4.0-4.1 kg, 10,000 km oil service.
usable_for_model: High-quality benchmark for MGU packaging, range, maintenance and automatic shifting scenarios.
feasibility_implication: Best production reference for Start.Select / Pre.Select / Auto.Shift user logic, but it is stepped not continuous.
open_questions: No public full efficiency map, thermal derating, shift-time-under-load map or complete sensor/fault logic.

mechanism_claim: Electronic actuation of internal gearbox enables shifting functions independent of rear derailleur.
control_claim: Auto.Shift, Auto.Shift.Pro, Pre.Select and Start.Select define scenarios highly relevant to eCVT MGU.
parameters: Target cadence, coasting preselect and starting gear are functional concepts rather than full parameters.
usable_for_model: Useful for state-machine design and rider override behavior.
feasibility_implication: Strong support for start ratio, coasting recovery and adaptive target-cadence logic.
open_questions: Discrete-shift window and torque interruption behavior cannot be directly mapped to continuous eCVT.

mechanism_claim: Bosch smart system integrates partner electronic transmissions rather than defining one drivetrain mechanism.
control_claim: Supports automatic/manual shifting, starting gear or freewheel shifting depending on partner transmission.
parameters: Partner-dependent; includes 3X3, Classified, enviolo, Gearsensor, Rohloff, Shimano, TRP etc.
usable_for_model: Useful as control-interface and ecosystem abstraction reference.
feasibility_implication: Shows OEMs can achieve automatic/electronic shifting without motor-internal eCVT, raising substitute pressure.
open_questions: Partner protocol, torque-reduction timing, error handling and exact sensor list are not public.

mechanism_claim: Officially positions motor plus transmission in one unit with ECVT route.
control_claim: AI-based ECVT monitors cadence, torque, speed and gradient to hold preferred cadence and efficiency zone; manual mode retained.
parameters: Official public claims include 120/150 Nm class, 1200/1500 W class, 400/500% range and connected services.
usable_for_model: Can seed target benchmark envelope, but use as claimed public product data, not verified test data.
feasibility_implication: Strongest current official high-power eCVT MGU signal for preferred cadence plus multi-sensor automatic ratio control.
open_questions: Need final X1/X1P datasheet, sustained power, efficiency/thermal maps, lifetime and real OEM bikes.

mechanism_claim: Reports new eCVT MGU direction at Eurobike and compares virtual gears/automatic ratio logic.
control_claim: Describes automatic shifting, standing shifting, low maintenance and quietness claims in event context.
parameters: Mentions Gobao 2027 production window and Avinox/Gobao headline values from public demonstrations.
usable_for_model: Useful for market signal and event timeline; not a datasheet.
feasibility_implication: Confirms this is an active competitive direction, but does not prove engineering feasibility under load/heat.
open_questions: Need official product documentation and independent tests.

mechanism_claim: Reports integrated motor/e-CVT concept with chain or belt compatibility, mechanical lock and regen/braking-related functions.
control_claim: Reports automatic/manual control and cadence/power-based behavior in concept context.
parameters: Reported values include 150 Nm/1500 W, 100-520% range; treat as media claim.
usable_for_model: Can define Avinox benchmark hypotheses, not validated model constants.
feasibility_implication: Supports feature scope comparable to user's product direction.
open_questions: Internal mechanism, continuous-vs-stepped implementation and final production limits remain unproven.

source_title: BikeRumor / Avinox Tech Details image OCR pack
mechanism_claim: Booth graphics claim torque smoothing, seamless shifting, configurable virtual ratios, single-speed external drivetrain and next-gen functions.
control_claim: Claims auto/manual/multi-gear shifting, fast shifting, load/standstill shifting and natural ride feel.
parameters: Image-embedded claims include 100-520% range, <0.1 s window, load shift up to 300 Nm, 45 dB, 3x durability and 10% efficiency delta; do not treat as verified production parameters.
usable_for_model: Useful for benchmark target list and validation-task definition, not final simulation constants.
feasibility_implication: High-value product-definition signal; shows what competitors are trying to claim.
open_questions: Need official press kit, measurement positions, test basis, final datasheet and mechanism disclosure.

mechanism_claim: Torque sensor and zero-point adjustment mechanism for human input sensing.
control_claim: Supports stable rider-torque measurement as prerequisite for assist and automatic ratio logic.
parameters: Patent-level mechanism; no current product parameter.
usable_for_model: Useful for sensor-state and fault-offset modeling.
feasibility_implication: Shows torque sensing and offset management are foundational for human-in-loop drivetrain control.
open_questions: Does not define eCVT ratio control or rider-feel objective.

mechanism_claim: Continuous or variable-speed transmission mechanism relevant to bicycle-scale ratio control.
control_claim: Can inform ratio trajectory and low-speed smoothness constraints.
parameters: Expired patent; mechanism-specific.
usable_for_model: Useful for architecture ideas and boundary conditions, not product proof.
feasibility_implication: Supports feasibility of bicycle-scale variable ratio mechanisms in principle.
open_questions: Efficiency, durability and modern e-bike load envelope not proven.

mechanism_claim: Friction-independent CVT family emphasizing input-output ratio control.
control_claim: May inform ratio linearization and actuation constraints.
parameters: Patent-level; no e-bike product parameters.
usable_for_model: Useful for mechanism options and ratio-control abstraction.
feasibility_implication: Shows CVT ratio control can be formulated mechanically beyond friction-only assumptions.
open_questions: Not enough to select architecture without efficiency/packaging evidence.

mechanism_claim: Two-motor planetary power-split architecture with multiple modes.
control_claim: Relevant to mode switching, controlled freedom degree and fault/limp concepts.
parameters: Automotive scale; high voltage, multi-clutch system.
usable_for_model: Useful for architecture analogy and mode-state modeling.
feasibility_implication: Strong conceptual support for motor-controlled ratio/mode management.
open_questions: Not directly scalable to e-bike weight, cost, voltage, cooling or legislation.

source_title: Transmission Ratio Design for Electric Vehicles via Analytical Modeling and Optimization
mechanism_claim: Analytical modeling of transmission ratio selection for electric vehicles.
control_claim: Shows ratio optimization should consider motor loss rather than assume one fixed ratio.
parameters: EV-scale model; can be downscaled with caution.
usable_for_model: Useful for optimal ratio trajectory and motor-loss approximation.
feasibility_implication: Supports evidence-based ratio optimizer instead of hand-drawn trends.
open_questions: Does not include human cadence/torque comfort or bicycle drivetrain losses.

source_title: Energy-optimal Design and Control of Electric Vehicles' Transmissions
mechanism_claim: Transmission design/control problem comparing CVT and fixed/multi-gear transmissions.
control_claim: Defines optimization framing for ratio, energy and constraints.
parameters: EV-scale; not e-bike-specific.
usable_for_model: Useful for OCP/MPC structure and sensitivity studies.
feasibility_implication: Supports simulating parameter windows rather than single curve.
open_questions: Needs rider and e-bike adaptation.

source_title: Virtual-bike emulation in a series-parallel human-powered electric bike
mechanism_claim: Human-powered electric bike with virtual-bike emulation concept.
control_claim: Human-in-loop control shows rider feel can be shaped by software if drivetrain is decoupled.
parameters: Series-parallel architecture; not necessarily legal/efficient for mainstream e-bike.
usable_for_model: Useful for human-in-loop objective design and virtual drivetrain analogy.
feasibility_implication: Supports idea that rider feel is controllable, but efficiency/legal trust risks are high.
open_questions: Not proof for mechanical eCVT MGU.

mechanism_claim: Bicycle power balance includes grade, drag, rolling, acceleration and drivetrain losses.
control_claim: Emphasizes instantaneous cadence/power interpretation for rider-side modeling.
parameters: Power-meter/GPS model parameters need calibration.
usable_for_model: Useful for vehicle+rider longitudinal module.
feasibility_implication: Necessary basis for climb and sprint scenario energy/torque calculation.
open_questions: Does not include eCVT actuator dynamics.

mechanism_claim: Provides bicycle longitudinal motion modeling and parameter calibration ideas.
control_claim: Can be used to build speed/acceleration response under rider and motor torque.
parameters: Bicycle dynamics; not drivetrain-specific.
usable_for_model: Useful for road-load and vehicle-state module.
feasibility_implication: Supports physically grounded scenario simulation.
open_questions: Need drivetrain, motor and rider comfort layers.

source_title: Energy-optimal Design and Control of Electric Powertrains under Motor Thermal Constraints
mechanism_claim: Powertrain design/control under motor thermal constraints and transmission choice.
control_claim: Shows thermal constraints can change optimal ratio/control choices.
parameters: EV-scale thermal model.
usable_for_model: Useful for motor temperature state and derating constraints.
feasibility_implication: Directly relevant to high-power eCVT MGU risk: ratio control may help motor, but thermal margins decide feasibility.
open_questions: Need actual e-bike motor and eCVT loss parameters.
